footer.description: DitakNet is a self-hosted monitoring server for individuals, home labs, small offices, CCTV, and local IT visibility. It runs on Docker, Linux, or TrueNAS SCALE on infrastructure you control.

home.metaTitle: DitakNet | Self-hosted monitoring for homes and small networks
home.metaDescription: DitakNet is a self-hosted monitoring server for individuals, home labs, small offices, CCTV, routers, and local devices. Runs on Docker or Linux on port 5833.

hero.title: Your whole network, visible — without the cloud.
hero.subtitle: Watch routers, cameras, PCs, and local services on a server you control. Free to install with Docker.
hero.supporting: Install once on Docker or a small Linux PC. Discovery finds devices, health checks watch them, and you keep the data at home — not in a public cloud.

audience.title: Built for individual buyers and small setups
audience.description: DitakNet is free to self-install for people who want practical local monitoring — not a heavy enterprise platform.

0.title: Home & home lab
0.description: Watch your router, NAS, cameras, and smart devices from one local dashboard.

1.title: Small office / shop
1.description: Keep printers, Wi‑Fi, POS, and CCTV visible without hiring a full-time IT team.

2.title: Installers & freelancers
2.description: Deploy for clients on Docker or Linux, then hand over a clear monitoring view.

explain.title: Built for local-first monitoring
explain.description: DitakNet runs on infrastructure you control. It fits home labs, small offices, CCTV setups, and independent IT work — without moving monitoring data to a public cloud.

problem.title: You only notice network issues after something breaks
problem.description: Homes and small businesses run routers, cameras, PCs, printers, and storage every day — but still guess what is online until a call, a blank screen, or a failed backup.

items.0: No simple local list of devices and their status
items.1: Manual checking instead of automatic health checks
items.2: Cameras and Wi‑Fi fail quietly until someone complains
items.3: No clear annual license for the tools you actually use
items.4: Cloud tools need the internet even for local monitoring
items.5: Hard to hand a clean monitoring view to a client after install

product.title: What DitakNet provides
product.description: A self-hosted monitoring server focused on real local operations—not a public cloud SaaS.

0.title: Network discovery
0.description: Identify devices and services on approved private subnets.

1.title: Device visibility
1.description: See hosts, status, and operational context from one dashboard.

2.title: Service monitoring
2.description: Run ping, TCP, HTTP, and related checks on a schedule.

3.title: Health endpoints
3.description: Expose system health and version status for operations review.

4.title: Alerts and status
4.description: Surface warnings, failures, and operational changes.

5.title: Backup and restore
5.description: Manage full backups, validation, and restore workflows.

6.title: License management
6.description: Annual licensing controls available product areas and limits.

7.title: Role-based permissions
7.description: Backend permission checks for admin, security, data, backups, and updates areas.

8.title: Update status
8.description: Check application version and update source configuration safely.

9.title: Local deployment
9.description: Designed for Docker, Linux servers, and TrueNAS SCALE targets.

deployment.title: Runs inside your infrastructure
deployment.description: The customer keeps the monitoring system on infrastructure they control. DitakNet does not require a public cloud dependency for core local monitoring.

items.3: Local network usage on private subnets
items.5: Data stored on the customer server by default

deployment.note: Implementation and environment review are available before production rollout.

securitySection.title: Local control with backend permission checks
securitySection.description: DitakNet is positioned as a self-hosted operations product. Security wording stays limited to implemented directions.

items.1: Role-based access areas for settings, backups, security, and updates
items.2: Backend permission enforcement for sensitive actions
items.4: Backup safety workflows with confirmation for restore actions
items.5: No public cloud dependency required for core monitoring

securitySection.caution: DitakNet is not presented as a finished enterprise security certification platform. Deployment review and hardening remain the customer's operational responsibility.

backupSection.title: Backup management with automatic weekly retention
backupSection.description: DitakNet includes backup and restore management for operators who need recoverability on their own server.

items.0: Full backup ZIP workflows with manifest validation
items.2: Automatic retention keeps the latest 3 automatic backups
items.3: Manual backups are preserved and not removed by automatic retention

implementation.title: Annual licenses for individuals and small deployments
implementation.description: Choose a package that matches your device count, install locally, and activate after a short review. New installs can start with a trial before annual activation.

items.0: Pick Lite, Standard, Pro, or Enterprise by network size
items.1: Install on Docker or Linux at home or in a small office
items.2: Request activation with your hardware ID and package
items.3: Product areas and limits follow the license on your server

implementation.note: Sales are handled as annual licenses for people and small teams. Complex multi-site rollouts still go through a short implementation review.

contactCta.title: Ready to buy or need setup help?
contactCta.description: Tell us your network size, cameras/devices, and whether you use Docker or a PC. We will reply with the right package and next steps.

items.checks: Ping, TCP, and HTTP health checks

itemDescriptions.network: Monitor approved local networks and services from one self-hosted server.
itemDescriptions.discovery: Discover devices on private subnets you own or manage.
itemDescriptions.camera: Check camera and recorder availability through network monitoring.
itemDescriptions.server: Track server and host health with scheduled checks.
itemDescriptions.checks: Use ping, TCP, and HTTP checks for operational visibility.
itemDescriptions.telegram: Send alerts to Telegram when configured by the operator.
itemDescriptions.reports: Review status, uptime, and operational summaries.
itemDescriptions.agent: Available in higher annual license areas where enabled.
itemDescriptions.languages: Operate the product in Armenian, English, or Russian.
itemDescriptions.docker: Deploy on customer-controlled Docker, Linux, or TrueNAS SCALE infrastructure.

pricing.title: Free to install. Pay only for professional work.
pricing.description: Install DitakNet yourself with Docker for free. Contact us only when you want professional installation, configuration, or custom development.
pricing.commercialNote: No annual software license is required for self-installation and normal use.
pricing.licenseEnforcement: You pay for our professional service time—not for a mandatory product license.

about.title: About DitakNet
about.description: DitakNet is a self-hosted monitoring server for local networks, servers, devices, backups, licensing, and IT infrastructure visibility.
about.what: It is a self-hosted monitoring server installed on the customer's own Docker, Linux, or TrueNAS SCALE environment—default port 5833.
about.who: It is for individuals, home labs, small offices, CCTV installers, freelancers, and TrueNAS/Docker operators who want local control over monitoring data.
about.why: The product reduces blind spots in local infrastructure by combining discovery, monitoring, backups, licensing, and permission control on one server.
about.vision: A reliable local-first monitoring product with honest licensing, practical deployment, and clear operational visibility.

deploymentPage.title: Deployment
deploymentPage.description: DitakNet runs on customer-controlled infrastructure: Docker, Linux servers, TrueNAS SCALE, and private local networks.
deploymentPage.body: Core monitoring runs inside your network. Default application port is 5833. Internet may be used for optional notifications, updates, or license communication—but the product is not a public cloud SaaS.

securityPage.title: Security and control
securityPage.description: Local-first deployment, backend permission checks, license-controlled access, and backup safety workflows.
securityPage.body: DitakNet uses role-based access areas and backend permission checks for sensitive settings. This website does not claim military-grade, bank-level, or certified compliance language that is not documented in the product.

contact.title: Contact DitakNet
contact.description: Ask about annual licensing, setup help, or technical questions. Tell us your network size, devices/cameras, and whether you use Docker or a PC.
contact.success: Message received. We will reply about licensing or setup next steps.
contact.implementationHint: Include deployment environment (Docker, Linux, TrueNAS or a home PC), approximate device count, and preferred annual plan if known.

support.title: Support center
support.description: Find installation help, submit a ticket, request a license, or read the documentation.
support.needLogin: Please log in to create and view support tickets.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.description: Guides for installing, activating, and operating DitakNet.
docs.body: This page is ready for detailed product documentation. Use it for step-by-step installation notes, screenshots, troubleshooting, and support references.

0.a: It is a local network monitoring platform for devices, services, alerts, and support workflows.

1.a: Core local monitoring should run inside the network. Internet may be needed for updates, notifications, or license communication.

2.a: Yes, the product is prepared for Docker and TrueNAS-style deployment.

3.a: The Free package includes basic limits for devices, one subnet or VLAN, checks, alerts, reports, and local deployment.

4.a: Lite, Pro, Corporate, Multi-Office, and Enterprise packages require a yearly license request and activation process.

5.a: Yes, it can monitor camera and NVR availability through network checks.

6.a: Yes, it supports server checks and agent monitoring in higher packages.

7.a: No. The intended discovery scope is approved local networks and VLANs.

8.a: Discovery should be limited to networks you own or manage and configured with safe local scopes.

9.q: How do I request a license?
9.a: Use the license request form and include your installation ID and package needs.
